---
title: "Acknowledgements"
---

## Communities and maintainers

This hub stands on the work of communities that build, maintain, and document the open scientific computing stack. With sincere thanks to:

- The [R Core Team](https://www.r-project.org/contributors.html) and [R Foundation](https://www.r-project.org/foundation/), for sustaining the R language.
- The [Bioconductor](https://bioconductor.org) project and its contributors, for the world's most coherent biomedical analysis ecosystem.
- [Posit](https://posit.co) (formerly RStudio), for the IDE, the tidyverse, and Quarto.
- The [Quarto](https://quarto.org) team, for a publishing system that takes reproducibility seriously.
- The maintainers of every package referenced on this site — the work shown here is their work, surfaced and contextualised.

## Contributors and supporters

With sincere thanks to everyone who has contributed to, supported, or made possible the work assembled here — collaborators, students, colleagues, funders, and the open communities whose tools we depend on. The breadth of this hub is only possible because of theirs.

## Visual and architectural inspiration

The structure, navbar pattern, footer, and curated-list approach of this site are adapted from [`boulingua/ressources`](https://github.com/boulingua/ressources). Credit to its editor for the design language. CTIR's hub diverges in being monolingual and in adding research-specific sections (CTIR packages, References).
